items.title: Bernstein taglia le stime su Kering: il rilancio di Gucci slitta a fine 2026
items.summary: Bernstein cut its Kering earnings estimates, now projecting Gucci retail sales will fall 4% in Q2 2026 and 2.9% for the full year at constant currency, pushing the expected return to growth to late 2026; the move echoes Barclays' recent warning that Kering's full-year guidance looks increasingly unattainable, with Gucci still about 40% of group revenue and 60% of operating profit.

items.title: L'Academy di Kering guarda al futuro, ma gli analisti al presente
items.summary: Kering inaugurated its Milan-based Academy of Excellence, pooling eight Italian training institutes under Pomellato CEO Sabina Belli to rebuild craftsmanship pipelines; the launch is framed against skeptical analyst commentary the same week, with Bernstein and Barclays both projecting Gucci's recovery slipping to late 2026 and citing Middle East-linked demand softness.

items.title: Oggi in Toscana e sciopero dei settori industriali e manifatturieri
items.summary: Tuscany's first-ever region-wide industrial strike took place July 9, driven largely by the fashion and leather-goods crisis: 830 leather companies in the Florence district have closed since 2019 (7,000 jobs lost) and wage-guarantee-fund use is up 250-260%; CGIL Toscana's Rossano Rossi called it one of the toughest phases in the region's industrial history, with unions asking major fashion groups (Gucci's home district) to keep production local.

items.title: Sciopero industria in Toscana: in migliaia sfilano a Firenze, nonostante il caldo
items.summary: Thousands marched in Florence on July 9 for the unified Tuscan industrial strike despite summer heat, with the fashion/leather district singled out as the hardest-hit sector; follow-up ground coverage of the demonstration described in item 003, relevant to Gucci as the district's largest employer and leather-goods buyer.

items.title: Il bivio strategico di Kering: la dote del tempo per Gucci e l'opzione Valentino nei modelli del sell-side
items.summary: Sell-side analysis of Kering's strategic crossroads argues Gucci needs more time to prove Demna's turnaround before markets extend further patience, and floats a possible Valentino option in some models; ties directly to the same week's Bernstein/Barclays downgrades on Gucci's recovery timeline.

items.title: Gucci Monte Carlo Ads Return, Burberry Heads to the Country
items.summary: Gucci released a second chapter of its Monte Carlo summer campaign shot by Mark Seliger, featuring Amelia Gray and Anok Yai in coastal-to-nighttime imagery, reworked Giglio, Jackie and Dionysus bags, and a 60th-anniversary nod to the Flora motif -- part of Demna's continued heritage-leaning activation strategy.

items.title: Gucci Unveils the Second Chapter of Its Monte Carlo Campaign
items.summary: Culture-press coverage of Monte Carlo Chapter 2 moving resort dressing indoors into hotel suites and villas; reception noted as mixed, with some praise for glamour and others criticizing the imagery as feeling less opulent and more staged than prior Gucci campaigns under Demna.

items.title: Gucci official Instagram reel: Monte Carlo campaign unveiling
items.summary: Official Gucci Instagram reel introducing the Monte Carlo campaign's second chapter to its ~80M-follower audience; part of the brand's own paid/owned activation wave rather than independent public opinion -- discounted accordingly per sentiment-rubric activation-coverage guidance. Like/comment counts not accessible without platform login.

items.title: TikTok "Gucci 2026 review" discourse aggregate
items.summary: Aggregated TikTok discovery page shows continued creator-driven review/reaction content on Demna-era Gucci, split between viewers calling the Fall 2026 collection a confident reset and others expressing disappointment that pieces feel repetitive or overly referential to Tom Ford/Michele eras; individual post engagement metrics not visible on the aggregate page.

items.title: Gucci.com avis clients (Trustpilot France, rolling reviews)
items.summary: Rolling French customer-review aggregate (French market rated roughly 1.9/5 on a related review index) with recurring complaints about product durability -- GG-logo PVC coating rubbing off, hardware tarnishing, and scarves fraying after light wear -- plus frustration over Gucci declining repairs on recently purchased items and slow customer service responses.

items.title: PurseForum thread: "2026 Gucci Flora"
items.summary: Active enthusiast-community thread tracking the Demna-era Flora reissue (Jackie in Flora nylon, Flora coated-canvas totes and bucket bags, GG Marmont in Flora silk twill); commentary mixes nostalgia for the print's 60th anniversary with skepticism about repackaging archive prints instead of new design, and price-versus-quality comparisons to earlier Flora runs.

items.title: Gucci Beauty License Is Transferring to L'Oreal in Mid-2027
items.summary: Continued trade follow-through on the Gucci-L'Oreal 50-year beauty licence (signed July 7, effective mid-2027, with Coty receiving roughly $400 million for early exit); still generating fresh sell-side and trade commentary this week as analysts debate whether the deal materially changes Gucci's near-term earnings trajectory versus the broader retail turnaround problem.
